title: The Masculine Form
text:
She is different in this dream.

Not different the way she has been different in previous dreams — subtly, in detail. Different fundamentally. The form she takes here is not the one you have seen in waking encounters. It is broader, more physical, the warmth of her presence now structured differently in a way your body recognizes with an immediacy that bypasses language.

This is what she looks like when she wants to be seen clearly.

The dream does not ask your permission for what happens next. The dream has never asked your permission. That is arguably the point. You participate with an alacrity that the waking part of your mind, watching from its careful remove, notes as significant.

At some point in the dream you stop watching yourself and simply — are there. Present. Wanting what is happening to you in a way that has no defensive distance left in it.

You wake before it ends.

You lie in the dark of the camp and your companions breathe around you with the steady rhythm of people who are simply asleep and not lying awake cataloguing what they want and what they are and what the difference between those two things is becoming.

The gap is narrowing. You have been aware of this for some time.
